message:
The other night, we had a surprise blackout that lasted a few hours. At first, I panicked a bit—where were the flashlights? Did I charge the power bank? But once I got everything set up, it turned into a cozy adventure. We pulled out the camping lantern and lit up the living room, and suddenly it felt like we were in our own little world. 

We played cards, told stories, and even made shadow puppets on the wall. It was a sweet reminder that sometimes the best memories come from the simplest moments. 

I realized how nice it is to take a break from screens and just enjoy each other's company. It’s funny how a little blackout can spark some low-tech fun! How do you keep the good times rolling when the lights go out? 

Oh, and I found this little refresher really helpful for next time!
